subject: Physics
solution:
Unit of force is written as, kg m s-2.

Now, torque can be written as, τ=Fs therefore its unit can be written as kg m2 s–2.

Pressure is given by, P=FA, therefore unit of pressure will be kg m-1 s-2.

Now, pressure gradient is given by ratio of pressure and distance, so its unit will be kg m-2 s-2.

Impulse is given by product of force and time, so its unit will be kg m s-1.

As we know unit of torque and energy is the same, therefore energy density can written as =energyvolume=kg m-1 s–2.

subject: Physics
solution:
Dimensions of $\mu=\left[\right.$ MLT $\left.^{-2} \mathrm{~A}^{-2}\right]$
Dimensions of $\in=\left[\mathrm{M}^{-1} \mathrm{~L}^{-3} \mathrm{~T}^4 \mathrm{~A}^2\right]$
Dimensions of $\mathrm{R}=\left[\mathrm{ML}^2 \mathrm{~T}^{-3} \mathrm{~A}^{-2}\right]$
$$
\begin{aligned}
&\therefore \frac{\text { Dimensions of } \mu}{\text { Dimensions of } e}=\frac{\left[\mathrm{MLT}^{-2} \mathrm{~A}^{-2}\right]}{\left[\mathrm{M}^{-1} \mathrm{~L}^{-3} \mathrm{~T}^4 \mathrm{~A}^2\right]} \\
&=\left[\mathrm{M}^2 \mathrm{~L}^4 \mathrm{~T}^{-6} \mathrm{~A}^{-4}\right]=\left[\mathrm{R}^2\right]
\end{aligned}
$$

subject: Physics
solution:
Given that the new unit of length $\alpha$ is chosen such that the speed of light in vacuum is $1\,\alpha/\text{s}$.

Time taken by light to cover the distance, $t = 6\text{ min } 40\text{ s}$

$t = 6 \times 60\text{ s} + 40\text{ s} = 400\text{ s}$

Distance between Venus and Earth is given by $d = c \times t$

$d = 1\,\alpha/\text{s} \times 400\text{ s} = 400\,\alpha$

Answer: $400\,\alpha$

subject: Physics
solution: The power factor is defined as,<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>cos</mi><mi>ϕ</mi></math> which is dimensionless, the formula of quality factor, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Q</mi><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mi>R</mi></mfrac><msqrt><mfrac><mi>L</mi><mi>C</mi></mfrac></msqrt></math>, which is also dimensionless, and the permeability in free space, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>μ</mi><mi>r</mi></msub><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mi>μ</mi><msub><mi>μ</mi><mn>0</mn></msub></mfrac></math>, but permeability in free space is defined as,  <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>μ</mi><mn>0</mn></msub><mo>=</mo><mfrac><msup><mi>c</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><msub><mi>ε</mi><mn>0</mn></msub></mfrac></math>, here <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>c</mi></math> is the speed of light in vacuum and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>ε</mi><mn>0</mn></msub></math> is the permittivity in free space so this having dimension.

subject: Physics
solution:
A. Spring constant: $F = kx \Rightarrow [k] = MLT^{-2}/L = ML^0T^{-2}$ $\rightarrow$ II
B. Thermal conductivity: $Q/t = KA(dT/dx) \Rightarrow [K] = ML^2T^{-3}/(L^2 \cdot K/L) = MLT^{-3}K^{-1}$ $\rightarrow$ IV
C. Boltzmann constant: $E = k_BT \Rightarrow [k_B] = ML^2T^{-2}K^{-1}$ $\rightarrow$ I
D. Inductive reactance: same dimension as resistance $= ML^2T^{-3}A^{-2}$ $\rightarrow$ III
Matching: A-II, B-IV, C-I, D-III.

As resistance, inductive reactance and capacitive reactance have same unit ohm. So, all three have same dimension.

Thus, RXLXC is dimensionless.

subject: Physics
solution:
As we know, angular momentum is given by, L→=r→×p→⇒L=M0 L1 T0M1 L1 T-1

=M1 L2 T-1

Torque is given by, 

τ→=r→×F→⇒τ=L1MLT-2

=ML2 T-2

Also, Stress ≡ Pressure =FA

Hence, [Stress] =ML-1 T-2

Pressure Gradient =dPdx 

Therefore, [Pressure Gradient]=ML-2 T-2

options: Dimensions of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>β</mi></math> is same as that of force.
Dimensions of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>α</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mi>x</mi></math> is same as that of energy.

Dimensions of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>η</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mi>sin</mi><mi>θ</mi></math> is same as that of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mi>β</mi></math>
Dimensions of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi></math> is same as that of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>β</mi></math>
solution:
Given that, η=αβsinθlogβxkT. Since expression inside the logarithm is dimensionless

βx=kT

Since the dimensions of kT are equivalent to that of energy,

β=kTx=Ex=ML2T-2L=MLT-2

Therefore, β has dimensions of force.

Since efficiency is a dimensionless ratio,

αβ=M0L0T0

⇒β=1α

Therefore, dimensions of α is not same as that of β. Hence option D is incorrect statement and the answer.

xα=βx=ML2T-2. Therefore, α-1x has dimensions of energy.

subject: Physics
solution:
All terms must have dimension of pressure $[ML^{-1}T^{-2}]$.
For $(h + Bt)$: Since $\rho gh$ has dimension of pressure, $[h] = [L]$.
For dimensional consistency: $[Bt] = [L]$, so $[B] = [LT^{-1}] = [M^0LT^{-1}]$
For $\frac{A}{Bt^2}$: $\frac{[A]}{[B][T^2]} = [ML^{-1}T^{-2}]$
$[A] = [B][T^2][ML^{-1}T^{-2}] = [LT^{-1}][T^2][ML^{-1}T^{-2}] = [ML^0T^{-1}]$
$[A] = [ML^0T^{-1}]$ and $[B] = [M^0LT^{-1}]$

subject: Physics
solution: $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{Q}=\mathrm{AT} \\ & \mathrm{I}=\mathrm{A} \\ & \mu_0=\mathrm{MLT}^{-2} \mathrm{~A}^{-2} \\ & \mathrm{P}=\mathrm{Q}^{\mathrm{x}} \mu_0^{\mathrm{y}} \mathrm{I}^{\mathrm{z}}=[\mathrm{AT}]^{\mathrm{x}}\left[\mathrm{MLT}^{-2} \mathrm{~A}^{-2}\right]^{\mathrm{y}}[\mathrm{A}]^{\mathrm{z}} \\ & \mathrm{MLT}^{-1}=\mathrm{M}^{\mathrm{y}} \mathrm{L}^{\mathrm{y}} \mathrm{T}^{\mathrm{x}-2 \mathrm{y}} \mathrm{A}^{-2 \mathrm{y}+\mathrm{z+x}} \\ & \text { Now; } \mathrm{y}=1 \\ & \mathrm{x}-2 \mathrm{y}=-1 \\ & -2 \mathrm{y}+\mathrm{z}=0 \\ & \therefore \mathrm{x}=\mathrm{y}=\mathrm{z}=1\end{aligned}$

solution:
V=K(h)a(I)b(G)c(C)d (V is voltage)
We know [h]=ML2T-1
[I]=A
[G]=M-1L3T-2
[C]=LT-1
[V]=ML2T-3A-1
ML2T-3A-1=(ML2T-1)a(A)b(M-1L3T-2)c(LT-1)d
ML2T-3A-1=Ma-cL2a+3c+dT-a-2c-dAb
a-c=1 .....(1)
2a+3c+d=2 .....(2)
-a-2c-d=-3 .....(3)
b=-1  ...(4)
On solving
c=-1
a=0
d=5, b=-1
V=K(h)0(A)-1(G)-1(C)5
